measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 4-5, Figure 4c
measurements.curator_notes: Approximate values read near the SWIR maximum of the champion-device curves.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 7-8, Figure 4B and caption; p. 11 noise methods
measurements.curator_notes: The 10 ns speed result was measured separately at 850 nm and is not transferred to this row.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 7-8, Figure 4e-f and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: The 18 kHz bandwidth and rise/fall times were measured under separate 970 nm conditions and are not transferred to this row.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 4, Figure 2c; pp. 6-7 methods
measurements.curator_notes: Detectivity is reported at 10 V/µm with stabilized noise above 1 kHz; no applied voltage or single exact measurement frequency is provided.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 4; Supplementary Figure 6f-i referenced in text
measurements.curator_notes: Measured room-temperature D* under reverse bias; numerical temperature and exact D* frequency are not stated.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 4; Supplementary Figure 6f-i referenced in text
measurements.curator_notes: Measured room-temperature D* in the self-powered state; numerical temperature and exact D* frequency are not stated.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 5, Figure 4b and accompanying text; p. 6 characterization methods
measurements.amber_explanation: Detectivity was calculated assuming that shot noise from the dark current dominates the total device noise.
measurements.curator_notes: Measured noise at 10 Hz was reported to saturate at the shot-noise limit but the published D* explicitly uses the shot-noise formula.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 5, Figure 4f and accompanying text; p. 6 conclusion
measurements.curator_notes: D* uses the measured noise current at 500 Hz and a 1 Hz noise-equivalent bandwidth.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 5, Figure 3d-f and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: Peak broadband D* and responsivity near the 1700 nm excitonic response.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 6-7, Figure 5a and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: Room-temperature result; the main article does not specify the underlying noise-acquisition method.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 6-7, Figure 5a and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: Cooled result; the main article does not specify the underlying noise-acquisition method.

measurements.source_location: PDF p. 5 noise methods; pp. 7-8, Figure 5c and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: The methods use noise at 25 Hz while the result paragraph states 140 Hz; frequency is left blank rather than choosing between the conflicting statements.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 4-6, Figure 4c-f and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: Room-temperature peak result; fall time is 6.35 µs, and numerical temperature and active area are not stated.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 4-6, Figure 4c-f and accompanying text
measurements.curator_notes: Room-temperature peak result; fall time is 65.71 µs, and numerical temperature and active area are not stated.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 6-7, Figure 5c-e and accompanying text; PDF p. 7 experimental section
measurements.curator_notes: D* is stated at the absorbing-layer first exciton peak; the experimental section identifies that peak as approximately 1250 nm.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 6-7, Figure 5c-e and accompanying text; PDF p. 7 experimental section
measurements.curator_notes: D* is stated at the absorbing-layer first exciton peak; the experimental section identifies that peak as approximately 1250 nm.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 6-7, Figure 7a and accompanying text
measurements.amber_explanation: Detectivity was evaluated from EQE and noise inferred from dark current rather than a measured total-noise spectrum.
measurements.curator_notes: Approximate peak value read from the published curve; exact area temperature and noise frequency are not stated.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 6-7, Figure 7a and accompanying text
measurements.amber_explanation: Detectivity was evaluated from EQE and noise inferred from dark current rather than a measured total-noise spectrum.
measurements.curator_notes: Approximate value read from the published curve at the plasmonic-device peak wavelength; exact area temperature and noise frequency are not stated.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 2-4, Figures 1-2 and conclusion
measurements.curator_notes: Noise was measured with a spectrum analyzer and was approximately frequency-independent; reported fall time is 1.4 µs.

measurements.source_location: PDF pp. 2-4, Figures 1-2 and conclusion

measurements.curator_notes: Measured-noise D* at 10 kHz; the 1 Hz field is the noise-equivalent electrical bandwidth used in the D* equation.

device.device_stack: Sapphire / ITO / HgTe CQDs (~400 nm) / Ag2Te nanoparticles / Au (50 nm)
device.device_notes: Approximate 1 mm2 optical area converted to 0.01 cm2.

measurement.source_location: PDF pp. 2-4, Figures 1-2 and conclusion
measurement.curator_notes: Noise was measured with a spectrum analyzer and was approximately frequency-independent; reported fall time is 1.4 µs.

paper.title: Silver Telluride Colloidal Quantum Dot Solid for Fast Extended Shortwave Infrared Photodetector
paper.notes: Ag2Te CQD eSWIR photodiodes comparing EDT ME and MPA surface ligands.

device.material_composition: EDT-exchanged Ag2Te CQDs
device.device_stack: ITO (70 nm) / ZnO (30 nm) / EDT-Ag2Te CQDs (236 nm) / MoO3 (10 nm) / Au (100 nm)
device.device_notes: Detectivity device using 1-2-ethanedithiol surface ligands.

measurement.source_location: PDF pp. 5-6, Figure 4 and accompanying text
measurement.curator_notes: Measured-noise D* at 10 kHz; the 1 Hz field is the noise-equivalent electrical bandwidth used in the D* equation.
